items.title: Woman suffers brain injury after 'toxic dose' of wrong drug

children.content: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

gallery.altText: A patient laying on a hospital bed, knees up with blanket covering their legs, holding a cup next to urine sample container

thumbnail.altText: A patient laying on a hospital bed, knees up with blanket covering their legs, holding a cup next to urine sample container

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Major upgrades planned for 200-hectare site once used as TV, film backdrop

children.content: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

image.altText: A wide angle shot of a small tin cabin nestled among trees in a rocky outback landscape.

items.title: 26yo man dead after violent disturbance in remote NT community

children.content: A man has died after a violent disturbance in Milingimbi yesterday, in which police allege some people were armed with edged weapons.

image.altText: A bird's eye view of Milingimbi

items.title: Jury hears 'no bad blood' before sistergirl's alleged fatal stabbing

children.content: Jurors in the murder trial of a man accused of fatally stabbing a 37-year-old sistergirl from the Tiwi Islands have heard there was "no bad blood" between them in the lead-up to the "family tragedy".

gallery.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

thumbnail.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a pattern blouse and serious facial expression.

items.title: Aboriginal affairs plan launched as NT goes backwards on Closing the Gap targets

children.content: The Northern Territory government has released its Aboriginal Affairs framework as the territory goes backwards on several Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: Steve Edgington wears a suit and tie while standing outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Pollution concerns at remote NT mine as 'rogue' operator stands down dozens of workers

children.content: Nathan River Resources has stood down more than 40 workers since Good Friday. Now financial, environmental and safety issues at the mine are being raised, as the NT government is accused of weak regulation.

image.altText: Bright orange water leaking into wider water source

items.title: School teacher who broke student's teeth found guilty of causing serious harm

children.content: A former Darwin Middle School teacher who struck a female student in the face, breaking her teeth, after she demanded her confiscated phone back has been found guilty by a jury of unlawfully causing serious harm.

gallery.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

thumbnail.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: NT Indigenous mining royalty fund's investment strategy questioned

children.content: Indigenous people in the NT are being offered the chance to tap into a $680-million royalties fund, but some analysts worry the money is being spent too cautiously.

image.altText: Two men look at an artist's impression of a new building

items.title: Pilot at centre of Matt Wright's trial charged with stealing croc eggs

children.content: Four men who were allegedly involved in a "sophisticated" operation to steal "hundreds" of crocodile eggs from Kakadu National Park have faced court in Darwin.

image.altText: A pilot wearing a headset with a microphone, preparing to take off.

items.title: Video shows father and son illegally scuttling boat off NSW South Coast

children.content: In a historic judgment, the two fishermen are fined $15,000 for towing their vessel out to sea and illegally scuttling it.

gallery.altText: Trawler sinking at sea as fishermen watch on from another vessel.

thumbnail.altText: Trawler sinking at sea as fishermen watch on from another vessel.

items.title: New data reveals age group spending up big

children.content: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

image.altText: A close-up of $100 notes fanning out from a wallet.

items.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

items.title: Why Australia turned to tiny nation to secure critical fertiliser supplies

children.content: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

gallery.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

thumbnail.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

items.title: Unemployment remains at 4.3pc, Viva Energy in trading halt after fire

children.content: Viva Energy is in a trading halt after a fire at its Geelong oil refinery. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Former US Marines pilot Dan Duggan loses bid to avoid extradition from Australia

children.content: Former United States Marine Corps pilot Dan Duggan’s Federal Court bid to avoid extradition from Australia is dismissed.

image.altText: Former US military pilot Daniel Edmund Duggan seen as a pilot in Tasmania

items.title: Soldier protest prompts review of recruitment in PNG defence force

children.content: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

image.altText: Prime Minister James Marape looks seriously past the camera. He's with a group of others, all wearing suits.

items.title: NRL has 'lost the fabric of the game' with six-again rule

children.content: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says the NRL has lost the fabric of its game, leading to anger over the league's controversial six-again rule change.

image.altText: NRL coach yelling instructions from the sideline, in the rain

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile found after being considered extinct

children.content: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

items.title: Quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and ER?

children.content: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor ER?

image.altText: Composite image of Noah Wyle as a young and older doctor

items.title: How this tiny town welcomed a 70-piece orchestra

children.content: The Sydney Youth Orchestras travelled to this tiny town to help inspire the local kids, but when they left, they were the ones who felt changed.

image.altText: Zhayle stands in front of a playground, holding a wind instrument.

items.title: Rebel Wilson's The Deb is a delight despite the ongoing legal drama

children.content: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

items.title: Treasurer says taxpayers to bear cost of pay rise for NSW nurses, midwives

children.content: Registered nurses will receive a "one-off reset" of a 10 per cent pay rise in the first year, backdated to July 1, 2025, and a 3 per cent pay rise the following two years.

image.altText: People dressed in scrubs, and a young boy dressed as a police officer, march in the street calling for a pay rise

items.title: Tasmanian government tries to avert parliamentary defeat of greyhound racing ban

children.content: The Tasmanian government is deferring debate on its bill to ban greyhound racing, in the hopes of eventually winning over enough upper house members for it to pass.

image.altText: A greyhound

items.title: Pilot in critical condition after light plane crashes in paddock

children.content: A man who was trapped in a lightweight aircraft that crashed in the NSW Southern Highlands has been taken to hospital in a critical condition.

image.altText: Small plane crash

items.title: Adam was quoted $60,000 for his home insurance. His local council is taking action

children.content: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

image.altText: man in blue workshirt walking along the river

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

gallery.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

thumbnail.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: NSW premier says accidental prisoner release is 'embarrassing'

children.content: The ABC understands Kyle Quayle was mistakenly released from Clarence Correctional Centre on Tuesday and is believed to be in the Newcastle area.

image.altText: head shot of a man with medium length brown hair, orange clothes

items.title: Two men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Irma Palasics

children.content: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

image.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.

items.title: As the dust settles on Gina Rinehart's huge court case, the reviews are in

children.content: Gina Rinehart and her company, Hancock Prospecting, are the "clear winners" of what prominent Perth industry commentator Tim Treadgold said was the "big daddy" of legal battles.

items.title: Ukraine looking to 'capitalise' on new momentum against Russia

children.content: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

image.altText: Soldier stands in a field launching a fixed wing drone.

items.title: Creators of fuel dashboard quoted by News Corp have links to Liberals

children.content: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

image.altText: A graph showing a plane, a fuel pump and a plane with a countdown in red.

items.title: Proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyer scheme slammed

children.content: Multicultural groups are concerned Angus Taylor's proposal to prevent permanent residents from accessing the Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme could deter skilled migrants from coming to Australia.

image.altText: Mr taylor, in suit and tie, speaks to the media in a passage at Parliament House.

items.title: Ticketmaster and Live Nation hold 'harmful' anticompetitive monopoly, US jury finds

children.content: A New York jury finds concert giant Live Nation and its Ticketmaster subsidiary have a harmful monopoly over big concert venues.

image.altText: The word ticketmaster is seen in white on a blue background, on an advert along the side of a sports field.
